sourceMovie.title: Runaway Bride
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: People revisit Runaway Bride for the same reason it broke through in 1999: clear stakes, memorable faces, and a romance rhythm that doesn't stall in the middle. Garry Marshall keeps the ensemble pointed in one direction. The comedy and romance blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: 27 Dresses
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Runaway Bride clicked for you, 27 Dresses (2008) is a strong next stop: both trade in comedy and romance, with comic timing and sharper banter. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Runaway Bride?
faq.answer: Try 27 Dresses, My Best Friend's Wedding, The Wedding Singer—each shares DNA with Runaway Bride while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Runaway Bride?
faq.answer: Runaway Bride (1999) is best described as Comedy, Romance. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Runaway Bride worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 6.1/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this comedy lane.
